_meta.purpose: Post-demo reviewer feedback (May 2026): the matching score must reflect that H-FARM is an applied / hands-on partner — not a peer for top research universities, and not a partnership target for Italian competitors. This file lists the entities where we surface a visible fit warning + apply a score penalty, plus the keywords that signal a high-fit applied partner.
_meta.notes: GEOGRAPHIC RULE (no pattern needed): every Italian university is auto-tagged italian_competitor. Reviewer's 'not interesting to have other Italian uni like Bocconi LUISS etc' covered the whole field, so we don't maintain a name-pattern list for them — Italy + university + automatic flag. The specific patterns below (Bocconi, LUISS, Politecnico, etc.) still fire so the detail-panel badge can stay specific where it matters; de-dupe keeps the penalty from stacking. Patterns match case-insensitive substring on the entity name. Each warning is gated by entity_type so a competitor pattern (e.g. 'Bocconi') doesn't false-positive on a student org named after the competitor university ('ESN Milano Bocconi'). Manual 'Hot' / 'Critical' / 'Up & Running' priority always overrides the candidate hide.
